title: Use Formula to sort data?
excerpt: I am currently using this formula to combine data from two columns: {Female Shoe Size} & {Male Shoe Size} so that I can create a stacked chart and filter by groups. However, when I set the sort order for the X-axis to as…

posts.author: SarahO (Sarah)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am currently using this formula to combine data from two columns: {Female Shoe Size} & {Male Shoe Size} so that I can create a stacked chart and filter by groups. However, when I set the sort order for the X-axis to ascending it will place “Infant/Toddler 12” before “Infant/Toddler 6” presumably because there is not a zero before single digit numbers. Is there a way to modify the formula so that data is sorted with single digit numbers first?

Capture 22255×1394 214 KB

Capture 11487×1245 33.1 KB

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@SarahO

Yes, there is. But I suspect that the easier way would be to add an extra space in the labels that have 1 digit.

If that doesn’t do it, let me know and I’ll give you the formula.

Jim - The Monday Man (YouTube Channel)
Watch Our Latest Video: The ITEM ID column - What most people don’t know.
Contact me directly here: Contact – The Monday Man

posts.author: SarahO (Sarah)
posts.cookedHtml:
@JCorrell
The extra space fixed it and I’ve made the adjustment to my board template. However, I already have data that I’ve collected via a form so I cannot alter the existing labels for a couple of boards. Could you provide the formula for me to use with those? I really appreciate it!

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@SarahO

You might want to check that you can’t change the labels. Status columns basically work using the label id’s. In most situations, the labels can be changed at any time.

posts.author: SarahO (Sarah)
posts.cookedHtml:
@JCorrell
You are correct! This board is generated by a form so I was trying to edit the label in the form view instead of the directly in the board view. Thank you!

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@SarahO

Great, glad you got it solved.
